div1.entryFree: Sneak, vb. to creep stealthily and meanly: s. not away, Meas. V, 363. a poor unminded outlaw --ing home, H4A IV, 3, 58. the weasel Scot comes --ing and so sucks her princely eggs, H5 I, 2, 171. what --ing fellow comes yonder? Troil. I, 2, 246. s. away so guilty-like, Oth. III, 3, 39 (Ff steal).
